case.question: Which of the hexagons labeled A, B, C, or D exactly matches the TARGET hexagon in the placement of its outer triangles?
case.rubric: Option C exactly matches the TARGET because it is the only hexagon with outer triangles on the Top, Top-Right, Bottom, and Top-Left edges, while all other options have exactly one triangle shifted to an incorrect edge.

planner_focus.weakness: finding the only exact match among near duplicates with tiny differences

case.prompt: Create one BabyVision-style visual logical puzzle image. Use a clean printed worksheet style on a white background, crisp geometric shapes, small labels, no photorealism, no watermark, and no explanatory answer key. It must be unambiguous and solvable from the image alone. Do not include the written question in the image. A clean printed worksheet puzzle on a solid white background with crisp, black geometric line art. At the top center is a flat-topped regular hexagon labeled 'TARGET'. Attached to the outside of the TARGET hexagon are four small solid black triangles pointing outward, located exactly on its Top, Top-Right, Bottom, and Top-Left edges. Below this, there is a 2x2 grid of four similarly sized flat-topped hexagons labeled 'A', 'B', 'C', and 'D'. Hexagon A has triangles on its Top, Bottom-Right, Bottom, and Top-Left edges. Hexagon B has triangles on its Top, Top-Right, Bottom-Left, and Top-Left edges. Hexagon C has triangles on its Top, Top-Right, Bottom, and Top-Left edges (an exact match to the TARGET). Hexagon D has triangles on its Top, Top-Right, Bottom, and Bottom-Left edges. No photorealism, no watermarks, no explanatory answer key, and no written question text in the image. Minimalist, flat, bold line art with labels clearly placed next to their respective hexagons.
